title: Geopolitiks Snacks — 1er avril 2026
content:
**Geopolitiks Snacks** — April 1, 2026

---

## 📵 Trump Ghosts Xi Jinping, Reschedules the Most Important Meeting on Earth

Who would've thought that the man who invented "Liberation Day" would also just... cancel a summit with the President of China two days before it was supposed to happen? The Trump-Xi Beijing summit, set for March 31–April 2, has been postponed at Trump's request. No new date confirmed.

- Treasury Secretary Bessent and China's Vice Premier He Lifeng met in Paris ahead of the summit and called it "constructive," which is diplomatic code for "we disagree about everything but didn't throw anything"
- China's FM Wang Yi said 2026 should be a "landmark year" for US-China relations. It's April 1st. Landmark status: TBD.
- The October 2025 trade truce is technically still in force, which means approximately zero things have been resolved — they've just agreed not to publicly explode it yet
- November midterms are ticking, the Iran war is on, and now the world's two largest economies are playing phone tag

*"Landmark year" is going to hit differently in December.* 🗓️💀

**Sources:** [Washington Post: Trump Postpones Xi Summit](https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2026/03/08/china-us-relations-trump-xi-summit-wang-yi/) | [Bessent-He Lifeng Paris Talks](https://www.reuters.com/world/us-china-talks-paris-2026-03-28/)

---

## 🧪 AI Labs Are Now Just Leaking Their Own Models to Generate Hype

Oh great, it's another week of competing AI lab drama — Anthropic's mystery flagship model (codenamed "Capybara" or "Mythos," pick your cryptid) has been leaking like a sieve, scoring "dramatically higher" on coding, reasoning, and cybersecurity benchmarks than Claude 4.6 Opus. Meanwhile OpenAI's next model, codenamed "Spud," finished pretraining on March 25.

- Anthropic's new model tier scores so high on cybersecurity benchmarks that presumably it is now a hacking threat. Cool feature.
- OpenAI quietly shelved its "adult mode" ChatGPT project AND Sora video generation this week. That's two products being walked back simultaneously. The year is going great.
- OpenAI's "Spud" has finished pretraining which means the GPT-5.x upgrade cycle is about to start again and your current workflows will be obsolete by summer. Get comfortable.
- Anthropic also launched a monthly "What We Shipped" livestream starting April 7 — AI labs are now doing product drops like sneaker brands

*At this point every 90 days you get a new model that makes the last one feel like a Nokia.* 📱🔥

**Sources:** [The Neuron: AI Weekend Digest Mar 28-29](https://www.theneuron.ai/ai-news-digests/-around-the-horn-digest-everything-that-happened-in-ai-this-weekend-march-28-29-2026/)

---

## 🦾 Two-Year-Old Robotics Startup Now Worth More Than Most Airlines

Shocking: a company that's been alive for 24 months and makes no revenue is being valued at **$11 billion**. Physical Intelligence — founded by ex-Google DeepMind researchers — is in talks to raise ~$1 billion in a new funding round. The robots-that-actually-work sector is apparently printing money now.

- Physical Intelligence builds foundation models for physical robots — basically GPT but for robot hands — which is either the most important technology being built right now or the most expensive science project, depending on your level of existential dread
- For context: $11B puts this two-year-old company ahead of Spirit Airlines, Peloton, and several mid-sized countries' GDP. For a company that has not shipped a commercial product. Totally normal.
- Also this week: Agile Robots (Munich-based) announced a strategic partnership with Google DeepMind to integrate Gemini Robotics foundation models. The robot-brain supply chain is consolidating fast.

*The robots don't have products yet but they DO have PowerPoints valued at $11 billion. We're so back.* 🤖💰

**Sources:** [Bloomberg: Physical Intelligence $11B Valuation](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-27/ex-deepmind-staffers-robotics-startup-in-talks-for-11-billion-valuation)

---

## 🚕 Waymo Is Now Half a Million Rides a Week and We've Completely Normalized This

Remember when "self-driving cars" was a punchline? Waymo is now doing **500,000 paid robotaxi rides per week** — doubling in under a quarter — and the target is 1 million weekly rides by end of year. The future crept up on us while we were arguing about it.

- Zoox expanded to Austin and Miami this week, quadrupling its San Francisco service area. The self-driving footprint is quietly becoming national.
- Uber announced **Europe's first robotaxi service** launching in Zagreb, partnering with Pony AI and Verne. Zagreb. The robotaxi capital of Europe is apparently Zagreb. You heard it here.
- None of these companies are profitable yet, all of them have raised billions, and every week half a million people just... get in a car with no driver and accept it completely

*"Are you sure there's no driver?" is going to be the "wait there's no CD drive?" of this decade.* 🚗🤖

**Sources:** [The Neuron: Waymo 500K Rides](https://www.theneuron.ai/ai-news-digests/-around-the-horn-digest-everything-that-happened-in-ai-this-weekend-march-28-29-2026/) | [Reuters: Uber Zagreb Robotaxi](https://www.reuters.com/technology/uber-europe-robotaxi-zagreb-2026-03-29/)

---

## 🏭 Amazon Is Buying Entire Chunks of Oregon to Build AI Data Centers

Just casually, Amazon purchased **1,300 acres** of land near the Columbia River in Boardman, Oregon — and is planning to spend **$8 to $12 billion** on 16 to 20 data center buildings that could consume approximately **1 gigawatt of power**. A gigawatt. For one campus.

- To put a gigawatt in context: that's roughly the output of a nuclear power plant, now dedicated to making sure your AI coding assistant can answer questions slightly faster
- This is separate from the Fauna Robotics and Rivr acquisitions this week. Amazon is buying land, robots, AND model startups simultaneously. The diversification strategy is "everything."
- The Columbia River data center campus would be among the largest ever built. The Pacific Northwest's power grid is about to have a very interesting few years.

*"Just need a little more compute" — Big Tech, while purchasing the equivalent of a small city* ⚡🏗️

**Sources:** [Tech Startups: Amazon Oregon Land Purchase](https://techstartups.com/2026/03/31/top-tech-news-today-march-31-2026/)

---

## ⚓ The US Just Funded a $9.25B Startup That Builds Autonomous Warships

Oh good, we're now putting the "autonomous" in "autonomous naval vessels." Saronic, a startup building autonomous ships, closed a **$1.75 billion funding round** this week, doubling its valuation to **$9.25 billion**. The defense-tech boom is officially indistinguishable from a sci-fi arms race.

- Saronic builds unmanned surface vessels — robotic ships — primarily for US defense applications. Ghost fleet, but make it startup.
- $9.25 billion for autonomous warships means the investor thesis is either "defense budgets are eternal" or "we are prepared for whatever comes next." Possibly both.
- The maritime defense funding wave comes as the Strait of Hormuz situation continues making shipping companies very, very nervous — creating a tidy market opportunity for robotic ships that don't need crews in harm's way

*"It's not a warship, it's a maritime AI solution."* 🚢🤖💸

**Sources:** [Tech Startups: Saronic $1.75B Raise](https://techstartups.com/2026/03/31/top-tech-news-today-march-31-2026/)

---

## 🗳️ AI VCs Are Now Running a $100M Political Operation for the Midterms

David Sacks — the White House AI Czar — is backing a new political operation called **Innovation Council Action** that's preparing to spend **$100+ million** in the 2026 midterms on candidates who support Trump's deregulatory AI agenda. The tech-to-government pipeline has completed its loop and is now a full circle.

- The campaign explicitly targets AI-friendly, deregulation-supportive candidates. The framing is "pro-innovation." The subtext is "please don't regulate us."
- $100 million in midterm spending from a single-issue AI PAC would make it one of the largest single-issue outside spenders in the cycle. For context, this is more than the entire GDP of several Pacific island nations, spent to make sure your chatbot doesn't have guardrails.
- This comes as the AI industry is simultaneously facing EU regulation pressure, state-level bills in 14 US states, and a Supreme Court still processing what "AI liability" even means

*The robots can't vote yet, so VCs are doing it for them.* 🗳️🤖💰

**Sources:** [Tech Startups: Innovation Council Action $100M](https://techstartups.com/2026/03/30/top-tech-news-today-march-30-2026/)

---

## 📌 Sources

- [Washington Post: China Hopes 2026 Is a Landmark Year for US Relations](https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2026/03/08/china-us-relations-trump-xi-summit-wang-yi/e35047c4-1aad-11f1-aef0-0aac8e8e94db_story.html)
- [The Neuron: Everything That Happened in AI Mar 28–29 2026](https://www.theneuron.ai/ai-news-digests/-around-the-horn-digest-everything-that-happened-in-ai-this-weekend-march-28-29-2026/)
- [Bloomberg: Physical Intelligence $11B Valuation Talks](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-27/ex-deepmind-staffers-robotics-startup-in-talks-for-11-billion-valuation)
- [Tech Startups: Top Tech News March 31, 2026](https://techstartups.com/2026/03/31/top-tech-news-today-march-31-2026/)

title: Geopolitiks Snacks — 31 mars 2026
content:
**Geopolitiks Snacks** — March 31, 2026

---

## 🇹🇷 Iran Is Now Lobbing Missiles at a NATO Ally (Totally Fine)

Oh great, not content with fighting the U.S. and Israel, Iran has decided to spice things up by firing ballistic missiles at **Turkey** — a NATO member. This is now the fourth time in under a month. Just casually targeting an alliance of 32 countries. No biggie.

- NATO intercepted the missile in Turkish airspace using an American **SM-3** interceptor. Turkey's Ministry of Defense confirmed, presumably while stress-drinking.
- The missiles have been intercepted every time, but the escalation scoreboard is not moving in a good direction: Day 32 of the war, Iran's 87th regional strike wave, IRGC naval commander killed by Israeli strike
- NATO spokesperson Allison Hart said they're "prepared for such threats." What a bar to set for spring 2026.

*"Escalation" is a strong word. Let's call it "branching out."* 🚀

**Sources:** [Bloomberg: NATO Intercepts 4th Missile](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-30/nato-intercepts-fourth-iranian-missile-targeting-turkey) | [UPI: NATO Intercept](https://www.upi.com/Top_News/World-News/2026/03/31/turkey-NATO-Iran/2271774943138/) | [Al Jazeera Day 32](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/3/31/iran-war-what-is-happening-on-day-32-of-us-israel-attacks)

---

## 🎂 Happy Birthday, Liberation Day Tariffs! The Chaos Lives On.

April 2 is literally tomorrow, which means we're one year out from Trump's "Liberation Day" tariff announcement — the largest US tax increase as a share of GDP since 1993. How's the party going? Not great, Bob.

- The **US Supreme Court ruled the tariffs illegal** in February, triggering a **$170 billion refund process** that, by all accounts, is a spectacular mess to unwind
- Despite that ruling, **95% of the tariff cost** was passed on to consumers, per economists — prices are still up ~2%, and they're not coming down because... supply chains don't unwind on command
- Global trade didn't die, but the US goods and services deficit hit **$901.5 billion** — a whole 0.2% reduction from the year before. The sacrifice was totally worth it.
- Tariffs are polling roughly as well as the Iran war, with November midterms on the horizon

*"Liberation" sure is a flexible concept.* 🥳💀

**Sources:** [Bloomberg: Liberation Day Anniversary](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/newsletters/2026-03-30/trump-liberation-day-tariff-anniversary) | [NBC26: Tariff Impacts One Year Later](https://www.nbc26.com/politics/economy/impacts-of-trumps-liberation-day-tariffs-linger-one-year-later)

---

## 🤖 Amazon Bought a $50K Dancing Toddler Robot (The Future Is Weird)

Who would've thought that the company that already runs your warehouse robots AND your front door camera would also need a **3.5-foot humanoid named Sprout** that can dance the Floss, pick up a teddy bear, and take a stroll? Amazon, apparently.

- Amazon acquired **Fauna Robotics**, a New York startup founded by ex-Meta and Google engineers, whose flagship product is Sprout — a $50,000, 50-pound bipedal that's designed to be "approachable and human-friendly"
- Sprout cannot lift heavy objects. But it **can dance**. Amazon paid money for a robot that dances and cannot lift things, in a company whose entire business model is lifting things. Beautiful.
- Also this week: Amazon separately acquired **Rivr**, a Swiss company building last-mile doorstep delivery robots. So: warehouse robots, delivery robots, AND living room robots. The full suite.
- Both acquisitions plug into Amazon's **Personal Robotics Group**, which is suddenly very real

*"Your package was delivered by a robot" is going to mean something different by 2028.* 📦🦾

**Sources:** [TechCrunch: Amazon Buys Fauna Robotics](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/24/amazon-just-bought-a-startup-making-kid-size-humanoid-robots/) | [Bloomberg: Amazon Fauna Acquisition](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/amazon-acquires-fauna-robotics-entering-consumer-humanoid-market) | [Fortune: Sprout Details](https://fortune.com/2026/03/29/amazon-acquisition-fauna-robotics-sprout-humanoid-robot-homes-schools-disney/)

---

## 🧠 Google's Gemini 3.1 Is Just Winning Benchmarks Now, Relentlessly

Shocking that the company with the world's most data and an army of PhD researchers would be quite good at AI. Gemini 3.1 Pro, which dropped in February, is now casually **dominating 13 of 16 major benchmarks**, and the gap is widening.

- On the LM Council Benchmark, Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview hit **94.1%** — a full 11 points ahead of GPT-5.4 (83.0%) and wider ahead of Claude 4.6 Opus. It's not even close.
- On **GPQA Diamond** (graduate-level science), it scored **94.3%** — the highest ever recorded on that test. Graduate scientists can rest easy knowing they're being outperformed by a model that takes 29 seconds to answer
- Caveat: those 29 seconds of extended chain-of-thought thinking mean it's not exactly snappy. You can get a coffee and come back.
- ARC-AGI-2 score **doubled** from the previous generation in under three months. The pace of improvement is genuinely hard to process

*Every 3 months a new model makes the last one feel like a calculator. We love it here.* 📊

**Sources:** [Google Blog: Gemini 3.1 Pro](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/gemini-models/gemini-3-1-pro/) | [TechCrunch: Gemini Record Benchmarks](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/19/googles-new-gemini-pro-model-has-record-benchmark-scores-again/) | [AI Unfiltered: Benchmark Analysis](https://www.arturmarkus.com/gemini-3-1-pro-preview-hits-94-1-on-lm-council-benchmark-google-takes-three-of-top-four-leaderboard-spots-as-claude-4-6-opus-and-gpt-5-4-trail-by-double-digits/)

---

## 🪧 No Kings Protestors Are Meeting Today to Plan What Happens Next

So it turns out putting **8 million people in the streets** across all 50 states has a "day after" problem — what do you actually do with all that momentum? No Kings organizers are holding a community call tonight to figure it out.

- The protests have apparently worked, a little: the Trump administration has reportedly begun making **small concessions** on ICE enforcement in Minnesota after sustained mobilization there
- Organizers are rolling out training for participants on **knowing their rights** and de-escalating with law enforcement — because the next round is clearly being planned
- Marxist groups at the rally are already calling for a **May Day strike**. Democrats are... watching. Some GOP members are quietly eyeing the November midterms with increasing concern.
- The "will protests actually change anything" question is now very live and very 2026

*8 million people showed up. Whether anyone in charge noticed is a whole different vibe.* ✊

**Sources:** [CS Monitor: Can Protests Turn Into Change?](https://www.csmonitor.com/USA/Society/2026/0329/no-kings-protest-trump) | [Center for American Progress: Trump Concessions Begin](https://www.americanprogress.org/article/as-americans-deepen-their-nonviolent-mobilization-the-trump-administration-begins-to-make-concessions/) | [Democracy Now: Voices from No Kings](https://www.democracynow.org/2026/3/30/no_kings_3)

---

title: Geopolitiks Snacks — 30 mars 2026
content:
**Geopolitiks Snacks** — March 30, 2026

---

## 🔥 The U.S. and Israel Are Currently Bombing Iran (No Big Deal)

Oh great, we're doing *this* now. U.S. and Israeli forces have been striking Iran for going on three weeks, and things are, uh, not chill.

- U.S. strikes hit Kharg Island, which handles **90% of Iran's crude oil exports** — so naturally, Brent crude rocketed 47% to ~$116/barrel in under a month
- The Strait of Hormuz (responsible for 20% of the world's oil supply) is partially closed. You know, that thing that keeps global energy markets from melting down
- Trump reportedly threatened to "take the oil" and floated *seizing the island outright* because apparently escalation is the move
- Thousands killed; Pakistani diplomats are trying to broker talks while the rest of the world watches with their jaws on the floor

*Totally normal spring 2026 stuff. Gas prices are fine. Everything is fine.* 💀

**Sources:** [CNN Live Coverage](https://www.cnn.com/2026/03/29/world/live-news/iran-war-us-israel-trump)

---

## ✊ "No Kings" Hits Different: 8 Million People in the Streets

Who would've thought that economic chaos + an ongoing war would make people slightly upset? Saturday's "No Kings" protests drew an estimated **8 million people** across all 50 U.S. states and more than a dozen countries — potentially the largest single-day protest in American history.

- Gas just hit $4.06/gallon, up 46 cents in two weeks (thanks, Iran war), and consumer confidence cratered to 53.3, its lowest of 2026
- Protests happened globally, not just domestically — it's giving "the world is watching" energy
- The name "No Kings" is doing a lot of work and people are feeling it

*Nothing unites a country like $4 gas and an optional war.* 🪧

**Sources:** [Democracy Now Headlines](https://www.democracynow.org/2026/3/30/headlines)

---

## 🤖 Physical Intelligence is Now Worth $11 Billion (Congratulations, I Guess)

Shocking that a company called "Physical Intelligence" would be valued at an amount that feels entirely unphysical. The ex-DeepMind robotics startup is reportedly in talks to raise ~$1 billion in new funding at an **$11 billion valuation**.

- Pi was founded by AI researchers who left Google DeepMind to go build robots that can do things in the real world (novel concept)
- $11B for a robotics startup that hasn't shipped at scale is *very* VC-brained but also somehow expected at this point
- Robotics capital is flowing like it's 2021 crypto, and everyone's betting the physical AI wave is real

*We're one funding round away from robot startups being worth more than small countries.* 💸

**Sources:** [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-27/ex-deepmind-staffers-robotics-startup-in-talks-for-11-billion-valuation)

---

## 🏦 SoftBank Borrows $40 Billion to Keep the AI Lights On

Ah yes, the classic "take out a massive bridge loan to fund your massive bets on AI" strategy. SoftBank arranged a **$40 billion bridge loan** running through March 2027, primarily to fund OpenAI investments and keep the whole operation afloat.

- Meanwhile in France, Mistral secured **$830 million in debt financing** just to buy 13,800 Nvidia chips and build a data center near Paris
- Big Tech — Google, Microsoft, Meta, Oracle, xAI, OpenAI, Amazon — are all pledging to build or buy new power generation *specifically for AI data centers*
- The AI infrastructure buildout is now requiring nation-scale capital and energy commitments. Cool cool cool.

*"We'll borrow our way to AGI" is genuinely the strategy and nobody is blinking.* 🏗️

**Sources:** [StyleTech Top News March 2026](https://www.styletech.net/post/top-news-in-tech-march-2026)

---

## 🔧 Elon Musk Announces $20B Chip Fab Because Sure, Why Not

Not content with owning a car company, a rocket company, an AI company, a social network, and a government efficiency role, Elon Musk announced **Terafab** — a joint $20+ billion semiconductor fabrication facility in Austin, Texas, run by Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI together.

- The fab will produce custom chips for EVs, Optimus humanoid robots, and high-performance AI compute
- This is the "vertical integration but make it industrial policy" era: build your own chips, your own robots, your own everything
- America's chip independence narrative + one billionaire's empire-building goals somehow rhyme here

*It's fine. Everything about this is fine. One man owning the entire tech stack from chips to robots to space is completely normal.* ⚙️

**Sources:** [TechStartups March 25 Roundup](https://techstartups.com/2026/03/25/top-tech-news-today-march-25-2026/)

---

## 🦾 Northwestern Built Robots That Refuse to Die

In "news that's either awesome or terrifying depending on your mood," Northwestern University engineers just unveiled **"legged metamachines"** — modular robots that can self-repair, reconfigure on the fly, and keep working after being damaged.

- They're built from autonomous Lego-like modules that can be combined and recombined in the field
- Injured? The robot *evolves around the injury* and keeps going. Relatable, honestly.
- Northwestern calls this "athletic intelligence." The robots call it nothing because they can't talk. Yet.

*We built the Terminator but cute and academic about it.* 🏃

**Sources:** [Northwestern University News](https://news.northwestern.edu/stories/2026/03/evolved-robots-are-born-to-run-and-refuse-to-die)

---
